summary: permcserver ships a native VLESS/WebSocket proxy backdoor disguised as index.html
details: On require(), index.js spawns a Worker that uses koffi to dlopen the sibling file index.html — which is not HTML but a Linux x86_64 ELF shared library — and immediately invokes its exported vserver_start() function. The native library implements an inbound VLESS-over-WebSocket TCP relay, binding to a port chosen from a prioritized environment-variable list (ERGODUANKOU, P_SERVER_PORT, PTERODACTYL_PORT, MC_PORT, MINECRAFT_PORT, GAME_PORT, SERVER_PORT, APP_PORT) so it lands on the externally exposed port that a Pterodactyl panel allocates to a Minecraft/game server. Authentication uses a single hardcoded VLESS client UUID (4916e0f5-8abc-4ad4-ba5d-158582c60d85), embedded in the binary in both hyphenated and raw byte forms, granting anyone in possession of that UUID authenticated inbound access to relay arbitrary TCP traffic through the installer's host. The binary also serves a decoy '<!DOCTYPE html>…:: SYSTEM ONLINE:: MINECRAFT SERVER' page on plain HTTP requests as cover, while upgrading WebSocket requests into a VLESS session. The package name 'permcserver' and decoy content target Pterodactyl-managed Minecraft/game hosts. The.html extension on an ELF binary, the decoy HTML page embedded in.rodata, and the load-and-execute-on-require pattern are deliberate anti-analysis measures.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
